dependencies: Cocoa products sourced from Far Eastern, West African, Central and South American regions
West Africa accounts for approximately 70% of world's cocoa bean supply
Single customer McLane Company Inc. accounts for approximately 27% of consolidated net sales
Approximately 74% of manufacturing capacity located in United States
risks: Commodity price volatility for cocoa, sugar, corn products, dairy, wheat, peanuts, almonds
Climate change and extreme weather impacts on crop yield and quality
Supply chain disruptions from natural disasters, pandemics, fires, terrorism, labor strikes
Concentration of manufacturing capacity in United States
Operational and financial instability of key suppliers and vendors
Third party service provider disruptions including cyber breaches or system failures

regulatory_risks: European Union Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) requiring extensive diligence on cocoa, palm oil, soy commodities effective December 2025
Changes in food and drug laws
Changes in advertising and marketing practice regulations
Changes in taxation compliance and requirements
Tariffs on U.S. imports and retaliatory tariffs
Import/export requirements for raw materials and finished goods
Changes in environmental laws and regulations
Introduction of living income premiums or similar requirements in cocoa sourcing
GHG emissions regulations and climate-related compliance requirements
Potential non-compliance with EUDR resulting in fines, penalties, or reputational harm

sanction_risks: Ongoing conflict between Russia and Ukraine may damage or disrupt international commerce and global economy
U.S. and other countries have imposed sanctions on Russia with potential for further sanctions
Potential governmental actions related to Russia-Ukraine conflict
Tariffs on U.S. imports and retaliatory tariffs in response
